authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Tahoe National Forest / Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit (Granite Chief Wilderness)

sources.title: Mildred Lakes (GNIS Feature ID 263565) — Placer County, California — HomeTownLocator (GNIS data mirror)
sources.quotedText: Mildred Lakes is a physical feature (lake) in Placer County.

sources.title: Granite Chief Wilderness — Wikipedia
sources.quotedText: The small lakes within the wilderness boundary are the Five Lakes, Mildred Lake and Little Needle Lake.

sources.title: Granite Chief Wilderness — Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Wilderness permits are not presently required for day or overnight use in the Granite Chief Wilderness. Fees are not presently required for day or overnight use in the Granite Chief Wilderness. Group size is limited to 12 people for day and overnight use. Stock are prohibited within 600 feet of all wilderness lakes (except to pass through the Five Lakes basin on the Five Lakes trail).

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Mildred Lake / Mildred Lakes not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Mildred Lake / Mildred Lakes not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Middle Mildred Lake (part of the Mildred Lakes group, GNIS Feature ID 263565) is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Middle Mildred Lake lies within the Granite Chief Wilderness (Tahoe National Forest / LTBMU), reached by an unmaintained use trail climbing from Picayune Valley near Mount Mildred and Mildred Ridge. No wilderness permit or fee is presently required for day or overnight use; group size is capped at 12 people; and pack/saddle stock are prohibited within 600 feet of all wilderness lakes (with a narrow exception for passing through the Five Lakes basin on the Five Lakes trail).

regulations.citation: USFS LTBMU — Granite Chief Wilderness recreation page
regulations.humanSummary: Within Granite Chief Wilderness: no permit/fee required for day or overnight use; 12-person group size cap; stock kept 600 ft from wilderness lakes
regulations.verbatimText: Wilderness permits are not presently required for day or overnight use in the Granite Chief Wilderness. Fees are not presently required for day or overnight use in the Granite Chief Wilderness. Group size is limited to 12 people for day and overnight use. Stock are prohibited within 600 feet of all wilderness lakes (except to pass through the Five Lakes basin on the Five Lakes trail).
